perturbation.original_text: This conversion will be carried out according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License, it being understood that all economic obligations of item (iv) above will be relative to the E. coli strain technology, not the Mitsubishi strain technology.
perturbation.changed_text: This conversion will be carried out according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License, however all economic obligations of item (iv) above will be relative to the Mitsubishi strain technology, not the E. coli strain technology, unless otherwise agreed in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces a contradiction regarding the economic obligations associated with the conversion to CB1. The original clause states that the obligations relate to the E. coli strain technology. The modified version reverses this, stating they relate to the Mitsubishi strain technology, which creates an in-text contradiction to the original intention. The addition of 'unless otherwise agreed in writing by both parties' further complicates the matter by introducing potential for ad hoc changes to the economic obligations, which is not explicitly allowed for in the section.
perturbation.location: Amendment To the Development Agreement - Section B
perturbation.contradicted_location: Amendment To the Development Agreement - Section B
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. New Section 13.10 is added to the Development Agreement as set forth below: "13.10 Notwithstanding the provisions of section 13.9, Bioamber may conduct the Scale-up and Production Project, subject to Bioamber hereby agreeing to convert the demonstration-scale Pomacle France succinic acid production facility and the Sarnia Plant to solely utilize CB1 as the biocatalyst for the production of succinic acid (and/or salts thereof). This conversion will be carried out according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License, however all economic obligations of item (iv) above will be relative to the Mitsubishi strain technology, not the E. coli strain technology, unless otherwise agreed in writing by both parties. In order to enable such conversion, Bioamber will put in place agreements with the owners/operators of the Sarnia Plant that will enable Bioamber to require such conversion of the Sarnia Plant to solely use CB1 for the manufacture of succinic acid as described above."
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text. The original and contradicted text state the economic obligations are relative to the E. coli strain, while the changed text states they are relative to the Mitsubishi strain technology. This creates a direct in-text contradiction concerning which strain's economic obligations apply, thus creating an inconsistency as per the provided definition.

perturbation.original_text: Bioamber shall use best efforts to obtain regulatory approvals for the use of the CB1 Strain in all countries where Bioamber and/or a Bioamber licensee are using any strain other than the CB1 strain for the production of succinic acid and/or salts thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Bioamber shall use commercially reasonable efforts, contingent upon available funding, to obtain regulatory approvals for the use of the CB1 Strain in select countries where Bioamber and/or a Bioamber licensee are using any strain other than the CB1 strain for the production of succinic acid and/or salts thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the commitment level required of Bioamber. Originally, they were to use 'best efforts' to obtain regulatory approvals. Now, they are only required to use 'commercially reasonable efforts, contingent upon available funding,' in select countries. This creates an in-text contradiction within the broader context of the agreement because if Bioamber claims to not have enough funds, then they would not be required to change their facilities, potentially, which contradicts what the section set out to do originally.
perturbation.location: Amendment To the Commercial License - Section A
perturbation.contradicted_location: Amendment To the Commercial License - Section A
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. Section 5.9 of the Commercial License is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Bioamber shall use commercially reasonable efforts, contingent upon available funding, to obtain regulatory approvals for the use of the CB1 Strain in select countries where Bioamber and/or a Bioamber licensee are using any strain other than the CB1 strain for the production of succinic acid and/or salts thereof. Additionally, Bioamber shall use best efforts to scale up the CB1 Strain and fermentation protocols utilizing the CB1 Strain." Nothing in these amendments will reduce Bioamber's obligations to replace MCC-17 and Bioamber's current E. coli strain with CB1 in all the existing and future succinic acid production facilities of Bioamber and Bioamber licensees, according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated 'best efforts' for obtaining regulatory approvals. The changed text weakens this to 'commercially reasonable efforts, contingent upon available funding, in select countries'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states, 'Nothing in these amendments will reduce Bioamber's obligations to replace MCC-17 and Bioamber's current E. coli strain with CB1 in all the existing and future succinic acid production facilities of Bioamber and Bioamber licensees'. The 'contingent upon available funding' clause allows Bioamber to potentially avoid replacing existing strains if they claim insufficient funds, directly conflicting with the unreduced obligation to replace existing strains.
